title: Local Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking
authors: Francisco Divi, Leonardo A. Lessa, Chong Wang
abstract: We propose a local notion of strong-to-weak spontaneous symmetry breaking (SW-SSB), through a local one-point fidelity correlator. Compared with the previous definition in terms of a two-point fidelity correlator, our local formulation offers two key advantages: (1) it is easier to detect in large systems: for a system of size $N$ and with ${\rm poly}(N)$ amount of resources, one can detect the local fidelity order up to volume scale $O(\log(N))$; and (2) the local SW-SSB order remains well defined in the thermodynamic limit, where the density matrix itself is not well defined. We show that key features of SW-SSB, including stability under finite-depth symmetric channels and long-range conditional mutual information, persist within this local framework. Our definition is conceptually analogous to local thermalization, as exemplified by pure states obeying the eigenstate thermalization hypothesis (ETH). For critical states, the local one-point fidelity correlator defines an interesting class of defect problems. We demonstrate the applicability of the local formulation through several concrete examples, and derive the universal scaling behavior of the local fidelity correlator in a range of critical systems, including ground states of conformal field theories as well as ballistic and diffusive free-fermion metals.
summary: This work introduces a local fidelity correlator as a robust order parameter for Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking (SW-SSB). This local approach overcomes the computational and mathematical difficulties of global measures, allowing for its application in infinite systems and complex models like free-fermion metals. It establishes that this local measure is stable and provides universal scaling laws for various critical phases.
main_problem: The paper proposes a local definition for Strong-to-Weak Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking (SW-SSB) to overcome the limitations of previous definitions, especially in the thermodynamic limit.
main_result: The local one-point fidelity correlator provides a robust, scalable, and well-defined order parameter for SW-SSB, which remains valid even when the global density matrix is ill-defined.
method: The authors utilize the one-point fidelity correlator, $F(ho_A; O_x)$, which measures the local deviation from symmetry, and establish its existence and uniqueness using tools like the Data Processing Inequality.
model_or_system: The analysis applies to general quantum systems, including pure states satisfying the Eigenstate Thermalization Hypothesis (ETH), free-fermion metals, and thermal paramagnets exhibiting various symmetry breaking patterns.
key_observables: Local one-point fidelity correlator ($F(ho_A; O_x)$), Conditional Mutual Information (CMI), and the scaling behavior of the fidelity correlator.
important_parameters: System size ($N$), region size ($|A|$), temperature ($ eta$), and the correlation length scale ($\xi_M$).
main_assumptions: The derivation relies on the fidelity measure satisfying the Data Processing Inequality (DPI) for the local order parameter to be well-defined in the infinite limit.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the local fidelity correlator, the tripartition used for CMI calculation, and the scaling behavior of the correlator in various critical systems (e.g., CFTs, free-fermion metals).
paper_structure: The paper develops the local definition by comparing it to global measures, proving its robustness (stability under local channels), establishing its existence in the thermodynamic limit, and finally applying it to calculate universal scaling laws in specific physical models.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful, scalable diagnostic tool for characterizing symmetry breaking in complex, large-scale quantum systems, bridging concepts from quantum information theory (fidelity) to condensed matter physics (SSB).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 2. Tripartition A|B|C of the system used to compute the conditional mutual information I(A:C|B).

FIG. 3. Schematic behavior for a pure state satisfying ETH. The one-point local fidelity F(ρA; Ox) saturates to a finite value at intermediate length scales set by the Markov length ξM (see Sec. III A), and decreases to zero when ℓ, the linear size of the region A, becomes comparable to L, the linear size of the full system. In contrast, the (global) two-point fidelity F(ρ; OℓO† 0) equals the ordinary two-point function and decays exponentially with a finite correlation length, ξ.
FIG. 5. Two coverings {A(n)} and {B(n)} centered at x.

FIG. 6. The tripartitions used in defining the local CMI I(A : C|B)ω = limn→∞I(A : C(n)|B) (left), whose charac- teristic length scale ξM, the Markov length, dictates the con- vergence behavior of the LFC F(ρAB; OA) in ℓ= dist(A, C) (right).
